candidates.stmt:
I’m south Essex born and a Billericay resident for thirty plus years.  In that time, I’ve been a long-serving local school governor, chaired the Essex Wildlife Trust and completed two terms as the Honorary Secretary of the Royal Society of Wildlife Trusts. So it’s no shock that I know just how vital all of our open green spaces are. 
I currently work for the Professional Footballers' Association, supporting former players living with dementia. I’m also a local football club director.
Like most local residents, I'm deeply concerned about the way that mass housing development is being foisted on our town without care or concern for local people.  Our schools, GP and other health services, roads and public transport are already at breaking point.  Without massive improvements in local infrastructure, our town will cease to function properly.  We simply cannot allow this to continue as it is.
I’m grateful to have been selected as a Green candidate for my town again - and together we can build a real voice for Billericay..

14 years of service looking to.serve again
Has completed 3 terms as deputy.mayor and 2 as chair of licesnsing and has been on licencing for 14 years
Billericay west is a big part of.my work and myself and my colleagues continue.to help residents please do support me again

I’m standing in Billericay West because too many residents feel the council has stopped listening to them. On the doorstep, I keep hearing the same concerns—parking pressures, inconsistent services, overdevelopment, and a lack of accountability.

I’m standing with Reform UK to bring a more practical, common-sense approach to local government. This isn’t about grand promises—it’s about focusing on the basics the council is responsible for: reliable services, fair planning decisions, and making sure your council tax delivers real value.

I’ve spent time speaking directly with residents across the ward, and I will continue to be visible, accessible, and responsive. If something matters to you, it will be taken seriously and followed through.

If elected, I will:
- Be present in the ward and available to residents
- Push for better accountability on local services
- Challenge poor planning decisions and overdevelopment
- Work to ensure residents get value for money from the council

Local elections should be about who will actually show up and get things done—not just party labels.

If you want a councillor who listens, acts, and keeps the pressure on, I would be grateful for your support.

Stewart will be a strong, fresh, independent voice for residents across Castledon & Crouch from Noak Bridge in the West to Wickford in the East.

Stewart pledges to be a visible and accessible Councillor working hard all year round, not just at election time. Stewart will campaign for a greener, fairer and safer Borough for all residents.

I have lived in Laindon for 10 years and currently work within Essex, London and Hertfordshire. My area of work covers parks and leisure. I have over 12 years experience managing volunteers and enjoy working with people from all different backgrounds. My own background is in Biological Sciences and I have a love of the natural world but most importantly the people who use and visit green spaces.

Chris Allen

Langdon Hills and Westley Heights resident for 28 years.

Chartered professional.

Previously elected to Basildon Council for Langdon Hills 2021-2022 and was a highly effective councillor the area.

Essex County Council is where so many typical problems arise: education, roads and highways, footpaths, lights, adult social care, to name a few.

Basildon Council also oversees big responsibilities, like waste and recycling, parks and play areas, local assets such as community centres and swimming pools, and not voting to disappear green belt and rights to elect.

Newsletters are part of the job when elected.

Identifying and implementing solutions to problems, IMHO, is superior to saying, "it's them, not me".

We have a nice slate of candidates in Langdon Hills, and Westley Heights, on Thursday 7 May 2026. But the risk of standing is that one of us will be elected to each public office.

If it's me you elect, then I'm ready to serve the interests of us all in Nethermanye and Langdon Hills, which comprises Westley Heights.

I'm standing in my respective ward and division to facilitate the option of NONE OF THE ABOVE for those on the Electoral register who wish to participate in the electoral process but feel that none of the candidates will represent them effectively. Should None Of The Above X get most votes, None Of The Above X would not take office. NONE OF THE ABOVE X is a mechanism to reflect numerically disaffected voters.

My name is Deepak Shukla, and I'm standing for election to be your councillor for Lee Chapel North on Basildon Borough Council.

I live in to the ward with my young family and am proud to be a member of this great local community. It would be a tremendous honor to be able to represent you and to put the things that matter to all of us in our area.

Every day I see opportunities for our part of the borough to be improved and made a better place for us all to live, work and learn. I am asking you to give me the chance to work on your behalf to make sure these opportunities don’t go missed in future. I will make it my priority to be a listening councillor. If you ever need my help or just want to tell me what you think, I will always be easy to find, be it at a councillor surgery, at community events, or by contacting me by email or phone.

I would like to thank you for taking the time to read the delivered leaflet on your door step soon. I very much hope that you will find it of interest and kindly ask for your support at the elections on the 07th  May' 2026.

If you have any questions about me or my campaign, please don't hesitate to get in touch. Yours sincerely, Deepak Shukla

I am a father and full time carer to my disabled son, I have experienced the consequences of cuts to public services and know that Basildon deserves better! I will fight for funding for roads, education and safe green spaces, as well as warm, affordable homes.

Like many SEN parents in Essex, I am concerned about the lack of resources and spaces at special needs schools. We are currently waiting on a SEND Tribunal in 2027 to contest the councils refusal to allocate my sons placement. SEND Tribunals rule 98% of cases in favour of the parents. In 2022, Essex County Council spent £566,299 fighting cases they know they are going to lose. If elected, I will fight to stop this waste of time and money and propose this money is spent on Class spaces. I will fight for you as parents to help you get the education your child deserves. Opening up more spaces in special need schools will then help mainstream education as teachers are spending more time on education for all children.

With my background of Automotive Engineering and mechanics, I am concerned with the potholes and the ‘fixes’ done in Basildon. I will fight to have our roads fixed properly rather than quick fixes to potholes that have come back when winter comes round. Our road are being neglected and require more than filling in the potholes. Potholes are having a big environmental impact with the increased amount of tyres and car parts needing changed sooner and the extra fuel used.

I will fight to protect our green spaces and help make them accessible for all. To make environmental conservation a key goal. Our green areas also have a huge impact on our mental health and wellbeing. I will help keep the local environment on the agenda to help keep us happier and healthier. Keeping green spaces available to all and safe is a huge priority to me.

Basildon needs more Council homes, which I will push for. Luxury homes owned by foreign business interests are taking money out of the Essex economy. Many people feel tha

Yetunde Adeshile is the Conservative Party candidate for Nethermayne Ward in the Basildon Borough Council, and Gloucester Park Division for the Essex County Council elections on 7th May 2026. 
Yetunde has been a local resident of Basildon for over 20 years and understands local issues, which is why she is standing as a candidate for the ward and the division.

Since 2014, Yetunde has supported under privileged people in the Borough, particularly young people, families and those from ethnic community. She has provided training and development opportunities which has helped participants gain higher academic grades, start meaningful employment, business or improve their business ethics. She delivers community cohesion projects that improve cultural integration relationships locally. She is part of the Basildon business community.

Yetunde is passionate about the development of our community. She would welcome the opportunity to be the voice that will help Basildon achieve the changes that we need for our Borough. It her passion that drives her dire to serve and work hard to move Basildon forward in the right direction. When elected, I pledge to serve Basildon community with integrity, dedication, and genuine commitment to our community. I will show up and be the strong voice that you need.

7th May 2026 Vote Yetunde Adeshile. The string voice for our community, advocating for a united and brighter future for Basildon, as we make Britain Great Again.

Not only is Craig the only Pitsea Councillor who lives in Pitsea, in this election he is the only candidate from Pitsea.

Craig has lived in Pitsea for 20 years and he is passionate about Pitsea and Bowers Gifford.

Craig is campaigning to bring back a swimming pool to Pitsea. The money for this has now been secured and if re-elected he will be able to make it a reality! Thank you to all those who have written to Craig in support to the pool. Please keep your emails and letters coming!

There will be 29 extra police officers for Basildon, which will include 6 paid for by the Borough Council and a crime fighting dog that sniffs out drugs and knives!

Pitsea will be getting investment of £2 Million in a high tech jobs in the new Innovation Warehouse at Wat Tyler Park. 

The Old Bingo Hall has recently been bought up and will be turned into a reception venue.

We are protecting Library services by re-vamping the Place and Pitsea Library.

Craig helped to regverse the planned parking charge for Wat Tyler Park, keeping it free for all to use!

We improved the Eversley Centre with a new gymnastic facility and 4G football pitches. We also opened new outdoor gyms in Pitsea.

As well as campaigning for a swimming pool, Craig is looking into the possibility of a motocross track to get bikes out of the estates and give something for young people to do!

Pitsea still needs a tidy up! We have invested in two permanent Pride teams to help do so and employed enforcement officers and put up CCTV to help name, shame and fine fly tippers!

Let's all work together to make a Pitsea a place to be proud of!

I’m Sam Journet, local business owner, community campaigner and the bold voice Basildon needs. From being homeless at 13 to building the highest rated independent jewellery business in Essex and Kent, I know what it takes to fight, fix what’s broken and deliver real results.

I believe in plain speaking, strong action and standing up for the people, not the party machine. I’m running to give St. Martins Ward a strong minded councillor who will work for you, not developers, not political insiders.

Tired of the same old promises? So am I. It’s time for local politics that’s honest, accountable and gets things done.

My priorities:

- Cut waste, fix local services and make sure your money works for you.
- Tackle anti social behaviour and crime head-on. More visible policing, zero excuses.
- Support local shops, jobs and our high streets.
- Bring real transparency to the council, no more backroom deals.

If you want someone who’ll stand their ground and stand up for you, lend me your vote. Let’s take back control of Basildon, together!

I was born in Basildon and lived in St Martins for 27 years, also worked at the same company in Basildon since leaving school. Just want to give you a socialist alternative and something different from the other parties
